prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿仰卧髋膝抬" (single-leg supine hip and knee lift) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a single-leg supine bridge with the LIFTED LEG TUCKED TO CHEST: lying SUPINE on the floor with upper back, head, neck, and arms flat on the ground; LEFT foot flat on the ground with left knee bent at 90 degrees — this is the SUPPORTING leg. The RIGHT leg is the LIFTED leg and is in DEEP HIP+KNEE FLEXION — the right knee is pulled TOWARD THE CHEST so that the right thigh is roughly VERTICAL (perpendicular to the floor) and the right shin is angled toward the head, with the right knee positioned ABOVE the right hip; the right foot hovers in the air near the head. Hips are lifted off the ground into a bridge so the supporting (left) thigh, hips, and shoulders form a diagonal straight line. Arms flat at the sides palms down. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the right knee is pulled high to the chest, NOT extended out forward, NOT lying flat.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus and hamstring group of the supporting (left) leg, plus hip flexors of the lifted (right) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: core muscles and erector spinae. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌 / 腘绳肌" pointing to the glute and hamstring of the supporting leg; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors of the lifted leg; "核心 / 竖脊肌" pointing to the core and erector spinae area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
